bull.b: Elicio observed rare confirmed complete responses when ELI-002 7P was followed by nivolumab, with persistent mKRAS-specific T-cell responses. This biological signal suggests the platform may prime resistant tumors for checkpoint response, a durable differentiation that can enable combination strategies and faster paths

Management’s pivot to a focused Phase 3 in R0 resected patients with extended dosing and DFS endpoint shows adaptive trial design based on Phase 2 learnings. Narrowing population improves signal-to-noise, can reduce trial size and timing risk, and represents a pragmatic path to regulatory relevance if biology holds.

bull.t: New $15M Registered Direct Offering Strengthens Runway
bull.b: The $15M registered direct offering, led by new institutional investors plus an existing large holder, provides near-term capital to advance planned Phase 1 development and platform work. Fresh institutional backing improves financing optionality and materially extends near-term runway to support clinical milestones.

bear.t: No Revenue; Persistent Heavy Cash Burn
bear.b: Elicio remains a pre-revenue biotech with sustained multi-year operating losses and large negative free cash flow. This structural cash burn necessitates repeated external financing, constrains strategic choices, increases dilution risk, and limits capacity to independently fund larger registrational programs without p

bear.t: Negative Stockholders' Equity; Weakened Balance Sheet
bear.b: Negative equity reflects accumulated losses and a thin capital base, reducing financial flexibility. With limited assets and modest absolute debt, the company faces higher financing costs and greater dilution risk when raising capital, and weaker ability to absorb trial setbacks or fund longer development timelines org

Missing the Phase 2 primary endpoint imposes durable program risk: it necessitates amended trial design and additional data to convince regulators and payers. Even with supportive subgroup biology, this outcome increases development complexity, raises costs, and delays potential commercialization unless prospectively v
